title: Simit Sesame Ring Bread

seo.title: Simit Recipe: Authentic Turkish Sesame Ring Bread
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best Simit recipe at home! This authentic Turkish sesame ring bread is perfect for breakfast or a snack. Get step-by-step instructions for delicious results.
longTailKeywords: Simit sesame ring bread step by step
seo.h1: Authentic Simit Recipe: Turkish Sesame Ring Bread
h2Headings: What is Simit (Turkish Sesame Ring Bread)?
Pro Tips for Perfect Simit Every Time
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked Simit recipe, authentic Turkish sesame ring bread, golden brown and delicious, ready to eat.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy warm, homemade Simit, the iconic Turkish sesame ring bread, perfect with tea or cheese!

description: Simit is a beloved Turkish bread, a circular delight generously coated with toasted sesame seeds, offering a unique crunchy exterior and a soft, chewy interior. This iconic street food is a staple for breakfast or a quick snack, perfect with a cup of Turkish tea or cheese. Experience the authentic taste of Turkey with this satisfying and easy-to-follow Simit recipe.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water, active dry yeast, and granulated sugar. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until the mixture becomes foamy, indicating the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the all-purpose flour, salt, and olive oil to the yeast mixture. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface.

instructions.title: Knead the Dough
instructions.text: Knead the dough for 8-10 minutes until it becomes smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. If the dough is too sticky, add a little more flour, one tablespoon at a time. Form the dough into a ball.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Place the dough ball in a lightly oiled bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until it has doubled in size.

instructions.title: Prepare Molasses and Sesame
instructions.text: While the dough is rising, prepare the molasses mixture. In a shallow dish, whisk together the grape molasses (pekmez) and 0.5 cup of water. In another shallow dish, spread out the toasted sesame seeds.

instructions.title: Shape the Simits
instructions.text: Punch down the risen dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a long rope, about 20-24 inches (50-60 cm) long. Fold the rope in half lengthwise and twist the two strands together. Join the ends to form a ring, pinching firmly to seal.

instructions.title: Dip and Coat
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 400F (200C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Dip each simit ring first into the molasses mixture, ensuring it is fully coated, then immediately transfer it to the dish of sesame seeds, pressing gently to adhere the seeds completely.

instructions.title: Bake the Simits
instructions.text: Place the coated simits on the prepared baking sheet, leaving some space between them. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until they are golden brown and cooked through. Serve warm and enjoy your homemade Simit!

tips: For the most authentic Simit flavor, use real grape pekmez for dipping. If unavailable, molasses works, but pekmez adds a distinct fruity depth.
Ensure your sesame seeds are well-toasted before coating. You can toast raw seeds in a dry pan over medium heat until golden and fragrant.
Simit is best enjoyed fresh and warm, ideally within a few hours of baking. Reheat in a toaster or oven for a few minutes to regain crispness.
article: Simit Sesame Ring Bread is a beloved Turkish Baking dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
